clinical_coherence.notes: 19F African American with PCOS, family autoimmune history (mother with SLE, maternal aunt with Graves), presenting with classic NMDAR encephalitis. The clinical picture is coherent. However, the PMH template (PCOS, asthma), family history pattern, and clinical presentation are very similar to S01 and S02.

tool_output_consistency.notes: CSF shows lymphocytic pleocytosis (74 cells), elevated protein (68.1), normal glucose (60.5). EEG with extreme delta brush. MRI with bilateral temporal FLAIR changes. All concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G04.81 correct. Critical actions clinically appropriate.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 19F African American is realistic. Young age is appropriate for NMDAR encephalitis peak incidence.

educational_value.notes: Third straightforward NMDAR case with same basic template. While the African American demographic and younger age add some diversity, the educational content largely duplicates S01 and S02.

issues_found: Third straightforward case with near-identical clinical template: PCOS + asthma PMH, maternal autoimmune family history, same EEG/MRI/CSF pattern
CSF profiles across S01-S04 are very similar (WBC 59-75, protein 62-78, all with oligoclonal bands and positive NMDAR antibodies)
strengths: Young African American demographic provides representation
Youngest case in the set at 19 years, near peak NMDAR incidence
